name: libqcanvas

metadata.name: libqcanvas
metadata.summary: Does most of the heavy lifting for qcanvas

documentation:
# libqcanvas

This module uses the API client module and does most of the heavy lifting. It is concerned with synchronising with canvas and storing/reading the data on/from an SQLITE database. 

## Using API result caching for faster development

Set `ENABLE_API_CACHE=True` (or `1`) as an environment variable. This will store the result of each API call on disk for re-use next time.
The cache is written to `/tmp/qcanvas_cache`.

Only use this when debugging.

variables.name: libqcanvas.database.types.canvas_database_types.RESOURCE_LINK_TABLES
documentation:
A dictionary for the tables used to link resources to pages/assignments/etc. 
The key is the __tablename__ of the content type.

Columns
-------
content_item_id : str
    The ID of the page, assignment, etc
resource_id : str
    The ID of the resource
link_state : str
    A value from ResourceLinkState. It must be a string, so remember to use .name

methods.name: libqcanvas.database.types.canvas_database_types.ContentGroup.items
documentation: Retrieve the content items in this content group.

classes.name: libqcanvas.database.types.canvas_database_types.Message
documentation: Used for announcements and course mail

classes.name: libqcanvas.task_master.task_master.Reporter
documentation: A reporter reports the progress of a task to a task master

classes.name: libqcanvas.task_master.task_master.TaskMaster

methods.name: libqcanvas.task_master.task_master.TaskMaster.report_progress
documentation:
Implementations can expect:
\- TaskID for a specific task to always be the same
\- For intermediate tasks to be reported as (current=0, total=0)
\- For total to change as the task progresses
\- For finished tasks to be reported as current = total (unless total = 0, in which case it is an intermediate task)
\:param task_id:
\:param current:
\:param total:
\:return:
A task master is the central entity that task progress is reported to from reporters

classes.name: libqcanvas.task_master.reporters.atomic_task_reporter.AtomicTaskReporter
documentation:
An atomic task reporter reports the completion of a task that is only 1 'step'.
For example:

tm = MyTaskMaster()
with tm.accept(AtomicTaskReporter("My task", "First step")):
    # Task started
    await do_something()
    # Task complete

classes.name: libqcanvas.task_master.reporters.compound_task_reporter.CompoundTaskReporter

methods.name: libqcanvas.task_master.reporters.compound_task_reporter.CompoundTaskReporter.attach
documentation: "Attaches" the reporter to a task. Useful for tracking the completion of tasks in a loop that submits tasks to a task pool

A compound task reporter reports the completion of a task that has several steps.
It can also be used to track the completion of many closely related tasks.

For example:
tm = MyTaskMaster()

urls = [f"https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/{num}" for num in range(1, 11)]

# Report the completion of tasks in a task group
with tm.accept(CompoundTaskReporter("My demo", "Make requests", len(urls))) as prog:
    async with TaskGroup() as tg:
        for url in urls:
            prog.attach(tg.create_task(get_url_async(url)))

classes.name: libqcanvas.task_master.reporters.context_manager_reporter.ContextManagerReporter
documentation:
Report the progress of a task where the progress is a number with an upper limit

Example:
tm = MyTaskMaster()

# Report the progress of some task with an incrementing number
with tm.accept(ContextManagerReporter("My demo", "Count to 10", 10)) as prog:
    for i in range(0, 11):
        prog.progress(i)
todo: merge into base Reporter, not really any reason to have this functionality separate

methods.name: libqcanvas.net.panopto.panopto_data_aggregator.PanoptoDataAggregator.get_course_panopto_folders
documentation:
Identifies the panopto folder for each course specified.
\:param courses: The courses for which to find the panopto folder
\:return: Dict of course_id -> folder_id

documentation: @dataclass

classes.name: libqcanvas.net.resources.extracting.link_extractor.LinkExtractor
documentation:
Resource ids are formatted using this:
<extractor name>{RESOURCE_ID_SEPARATOR}<resource id>
E.g. canvas$12341234

classes.name: libqcanvas.net.sync._canvas_data_bundle.CanvasDataBundle
documentation: A CanvasDataBundle is a collection of various data retrieved from canvas

classes.name: libqcanvas.net.resources.scanning.resource_scanner.ResourceScanner
documentation: A ResourceScanner uses Extractors to extract resources from course content

classes.name: libqcanvas.net.sync.canvas_sync_observer.CanvasSyncObserver
documentation: A CanvasSyncObserver subscribes to events related to synchronisation from canvas

classes.name: libqcanvas.net.sync.canvas_sync_observer.CanvasSyncObservable
documentation: A CanvasSyncObservable publishes events related to synchronisation from canvas

classes.name: libqcanvas.net.sync.synchronization_client.SynchronizationClient
documentation:
A SynchronizationClient uses a CanvasClient to retrieve data from canvas and a ResourceScanner to extract resources
from each course. This data is added to the QCanvasDatabase.

classes.name: libqcanvas.net.sync.data_adapter.DataAdapter
documentation: A DataAdapter deeply iterates over everything in a CanvasDataBundle and adds this data to the database using an APIDataImporter

functions.name: libqcanvas.alembic.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.
other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.

functions.name: libqcanvas.alembic.env.run_async_migrations
documentation:
In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.

functions.name: libqcanvas.alembic.env.run_migrations_online
documentation: Run migrations in 'online' mode.

modules.name: libqcanvas.gql_queries.single_course
documentation: Generated by qenerate plugin=pydantic_v2. DO NOT MODIFY MANUALLY!

functions.name: libqcanvas.gql_queries.single_course.query
documentation:
This is a convenience function which queries and parses the data into
concrete types. It should be compatible with most GQL clients.
You do not have to use it to consume the generated data classes.
Alternatively, you can also mime and alternate the behavior
of this function in the caller.

Parameters:
    query_func (Callable): Function which queries your GQL Server
    kwargs: optional arguments that will be passed to the query function

Returns:
    SingleCourseQueryData: queried data parsed into generated classes

classes.name: libqcanvas.util.dev_failed_resource_list.FailedResourcesList
documentation:
Meant to be used as a development convenience only:
Remembers if a resource couldn't be retrieved, so we don't bother getting it again. This is saved to disk.
Should NOT be used for release builds!

functions.name: libqcanvas.util.timezone_util.as_utc
documentation:
Converts a time to UTC.
If the time has no time zone, it is assumed to be in UTC already. If it does have a timezone, it is converted to UTC.
If the time is None, None is returned.
\:param time: The time to convert.
\:return: The time as UTC, or None

functions.name: libqcanvas.util.timezone_util.as_local
documentation:
Converts a foreign time to local time.
If the time has no timezone, it is assumed to be in UTC. If it does have a timezone, it is converted appropriately.
If the time is None, None is returned.
\:param time: The time to convert.
\:return: The time as local time, or None

functions.name: libqcanvas.util.canvas_data_sanitiser.remove_unwanted_whitespaces
documentation:
Remove trailing spaces and replace non-breaking spaces
\:param contaminated_data: The string to clean
\:return: The sanitized string
